calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-02-2020 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-01-2020 Advanced From Jul-20-20 To Jun-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington appeared by telephone for Dennis Bradford (Petitioner).
Ernest Der appeared by telephone for Debra Dolch (Petitioner).
Constance Figuers appeared by telephone for Lolita Newt, Loretha Bradford and Tasha Bradford.

The Court asked if the Parties had anything to add concerning the extraordinary fee requests. Ms. Harrington stated that her Client is the only surviving Child of the Decedent and had priority to serve, that they got a Professional appointed so that they could get the estate resolved and that her Client is the majority Beneficiary. Ms. Figuers stated that it has been their position from the beginning that Mr. Bradford was unfit to serve and nominate. The Parties indicated that they had nothing further to add regarding extraordinary fees. The Court noted that this matter was advanced on the representation that everything was worked out, stated that it looked at the papers and in fact it is not worked out, that Ms. Dolch takes no position, that the Parties are putting the responsibility on the Court to read voluminous fee records and that if there is an extraordinary fee request, the Personal Representative is in the best position to give some view on this. The Court also questions how this is different from a Personal Representative's responsibility to address creditor's claims. Ms. Harrington stated that Ms. Dolch was not involved until after the trial, that her Client/majority Beneficiary approves of the fees and that all of the Beneficiaries were noticed. The Court addressed the assignment issue stating that it only has a short declaration from Loretha saying that she has assigned the remainder of her interest, that there is no agreement and that there is no information regarding the consideration given by the firm, or circumstances of the agreement. Ms. Figuers stated that Ms. Bradford was concerned about the payment of their fees and agreed to assign her remainder interest to ensure that their fees would be paid, and that if the assignment is more than the fees, Loretha would receive the difference. The Court questions whether the amount of the assignment is in addition to the statutory fees and notes that the assignment does not appear to conform to Probate Code Section 11604. Ms. Figuers stated that the fees are the same, that there are no other fees and that the Court has discretion to award fees, or not. Ms. Harrington stated that she feels it is inappropriate for any Attorney to be awarded double fees. Discussion was held regarding the declaration filed by Ms. Figuers that refers to Judge Stewart's failure to render a decision after the trial. The Court noted that the minutes from May 29, 2019 reflect that the petition filed by Ms. Figuers' Clients was denied. Ms. Harrington stated that the confusion about the procedures in Probate was part of the reason why they had a trial. The Court provided the following options to Ms. Figures: 1) withdraw her request for payment of the assignment, or 2) continue the matter to give Ms. Figuers time to provide the agreement, declarations regarding circumstances surrounding execution of the agreement and the consideration given by the law firm in exchange for the assignment for the Court to determine whether it conforms with Probate Code 11604. Ms. Figuers elected to withdraw her request for payment of the assignment. Mr. Der stated that he provided a proposed order on May 28th that is consistent with the last amendment. The Amended First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1526 Hudson Avenue, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Administrator)

The sale was confirmed at $1,352,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). No additional bond is required. Mr. Der stated the current bond of $2,000,000.00 is over-bonded.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1526 Hudson Avenue, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Sep-30-2019 Continued To Oct-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch.

The Court stated before it today is the Report of Sale and Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property at 1526 Hudson Avenue, San Francisco, CA; 1528 Hudson Avenue, San Francisco, CA. Mr. Der stated that with full authority that they are not required to follow the procedures with regard to publication. The Court presented its concern with regard to publication, stated that it would sign an order shortening notice for publication, and continued the matter two weeks. Mr. Der is to prepare the order shortening time regarding publication. The Report of Sale and Petition to Confirm of Sale of Real Property at at 1526 Hudson Avenue, San Francisco, CA; 1528 Hudson Avenue, San Francisco, CA is continued to October 7, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Probate Examiner: Probate Director Sandy Hilton. Deputy: E. Lopes. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION WITH GENERAL POWERS
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-07-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Amy Harrington for and with Petitioner: Dennis Bradford
Michael Neham for Objectors/Petitioners: Tasha Bradford, Lillita Newt, and Loretha Bradford
Ernest Der for Nominated Administrator: Debra Dolch

The Court inquired if Ms. Dolch has filed a consent. Mr. Der responded that she has not filed yet, but she will accept. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to having a professional fiduciary, Mr. Dennis Bradford's standing to nominate, Mr. Dennis Bradford's nominee having conflict with estate. Mr. Neham requests time for written objection. The Court notes they already had 30 days to object.

Tasha Bradford, Lillita Newt, and Loretha Bradford's Petition for Letters of Administration is DENIED.
Dennis Bradford's Petition for Letters of Administration is Granted (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Text Ruling

Ex Parte Application For Order After Trial And Letters Of Administration, Or Alternatively, Special Administration And To Shorten Time; Memorandum In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-20-2019 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-29-2019 Continued To May-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-29-2019 Continued To May-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for Dennis Bradford.
Michael Neham for Lolita Newt, Loretha Bradford and Tasha Bradford.

Ms. Harrington stated that she communicated to Counsel that her Client would be willing to serve with his Client, that Counsel came back with a settlement regarding rents, etc., spoke of the possibility of the parties living in the house having tenants rights and stated that she could not agree to the settlement. Ms. Harrington also stated that she only represents her Client as an Administrator, not a Beneficiary, that she is not prepared to do anything with regard to tenants rights and that her Client is fine with having a Professional. Mr. Neham stated that he would agree to working with Mr. Bradford provided there is an order that includes language that he intends to move out by a date certain. Ms. Harrington stated that she will not agree to that. The Court informed Counsel that the parties need to select a neutral fiduciary. The Petitions for Letters of Administration (2) are continued to May 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Harrington will file an amended petition with the selection of the neutral fiduciary.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Apr-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Apr-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Neham for Tasha Bradford, Laretha Bradford and Lolita Newt.
Amy Harrington for and with Dennis Bradford.
Zennaita Bradford in Propria Persona.

Ms. Harrington stated that her Client obtained a $20,000.00 loan and is actively looking for housing, that he paid $1,228.00 for the property insurance and the PG&E bill on his own, and that he has spoken with the other occupants of the house and has informed them that he expects them to vacate the premises. Mr. Neham requested a 120-day continuance to conduct discovery and setting of an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Bradford asked the Court to hold the money from the sale and to have distribution made by the Court, stated that the property has not been updated since the nineteen hundreds with respect to the number of rooms, and that she spoke with someone from the Assessor's Office and they are willing to come out and update it. Ms. Harrington raised concern regarding multiple parties acting on behalf of the estate. The Court informed Ms. Bradford that no money can be distributed without a Court order. The Petitions for Letters of Administration (2) is continued to April 5, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a one half day evidentiary hearing. Trial briefs, witness lists and exhibit lists are to be filed by March 29, 2019.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-10-2018 Continued To Jan-28-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Micheal Neham for Tasha Bradford, Loretha Bradford and Lolita Newt (Petitioners/Objectors).
Amy Harrington appeared by telephone for Dennis Bradford (original Petitioner).

The Court noted that Ms. Harrington's Client has statutory priority. Ms. Harrington stated that she objects to the Demiris Firm's representation of the Administrator and some of the Beneficiaries, and that it is an ethical conflict. Mr. Neham stated that he is not prepared to address the issue of conflict. Ms. Harrington stated that Dennis, some of the Nephews and Nieces, and there romantic partners are all occupants of the property, and that they understand that they need to vacate the premises for sale. The Court stated that it is concerned about appointing Dennis as the Administrator when he is in the premises and questions whether he can move out within the two months. Ms. Harrington stated that there is a house and a vacant lot, that Dennis is entitled to one third of the estate, that he can request an advance on his inheritance to move out and that all of Mr. Demiris' Client's have been given a loan in the amount of $25,000.00 on their inheritances. The Court stated that it is not inclined to appoint Ms. Harrington's Client while he is living in the house. Ms. Harrington stated that the Beneficiaries have no obligation to pay any bills on the house, that all of the people living there are paying the bills, that there is no cash in the estate, that the property taxes are not being paid, that there will be a 10% penalty and that property taxes will be paid out of escrow. The Court stated that it does not believe there is a conflict until Mr. Demiris' Clients are appointed and that they can substitute other Counsel in if needed. Mr. Neham requested an evidentiary hearing and stated that he does not believe Dennis is fit to be the Administrator. The Court denied Mr. Neham's request for an evidentiary hearing. The Petitions for Letters of Administration (2) are continued to January 28, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Neham is to file a reply by January 2, 2019. Ms. Harrington is to file her opposition about the evidentiary hearing by January 11, 2019.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Continued To Nov-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Amy Harrington for and with Dennis Bradford.
Constance Figuers for Lolita Newt, Loretha Bradford, and Tasha Bradford.

Ms. Figuers stated that originally that Loretha Bradford was nominated but developed health issues, that Lolita Newt was nominated, that she asked if the other side would stipulate, and that request was denied. Ms. Figuers addressed the issue with regard to publication stating that when she contacted the clerk she didn't have three days for a continuance, that she has to have the hearing date before she can do the publication, and that she needed the continuance to adequately serve. Ms. Harrington stated that she wanted to object to the new administrator. Discussions regarding an evidentiary hearing are held. At the conclusion of discussions, Ms. Harrington addressed the issue with regard to Mr. Konstantine Demiris representing the beneficiaries and the proposed administrator stating that the adminstrator would need to get new counsel, and that her client will not waive the conflict. Discussions regarding the filing of an amended petition and a substitution of attorney are held. The Court continued the petition to November 26, 2018 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 for status on the conflict of interest.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-19-2018 Continued To Oct-03-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington for and with Dennis Bradford (Petitioner).
Constance Figuers for and with Loretha Bradford, Tasha Bradford and for Lolita Newt (Granddaughters).

This was a pre-granted matter. Ms. Figuers stated that she is objecting under Probate Code 1043, that she filed written objections and that she filed a competing petition. Ms. Figuers also stated that they believe Mr. Bradford is not fit to handle the estate, that he is insolvent, that there is a conflict, that he is living in the property rent free, etc. Ms. Harrington stated that her Client is prepared to file a bond, that she has the bond with her, that her Client has priority as he is the Decedent's only living Child and that all of the allegations are about future acts. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to October 3, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. This petition will then be continued to the hearing date assigned to Ms. Figuers' petition.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
